feed.title: Light Tower Group Insights
feed.subtitle: Institutional capital markets analysis, deal structuring strategy, and CRE market intelligence from Light Tower Group.
feed.rights: Â© 2026 Light Tower Group. All rights reserved.

entry.title: How to Choose a CRE Capital Advisor: 7 Questions That Separate Good Brokers From Great Ones
entry.summary: Before you sign an engagement letter, ask these 7 questions. The difference between a fee-collector and a true capital markets advisor can cost you millions. A field guide to evaluating CRE capital brokers and advisors.

entry.title: How Lenders Calculate Maximum Loan Size: NOI, Debt Yield, and DSCR Working Together
entry.summary: Three constraints determine your maximum loan amount: LTV, DSCR, and Debt Yield. The binding constraint is always the lowest of the three. Includes worked examples, rate sensitivity analysis, and a lender-by-type comparison table.

entry.title: Debt Yield vs. LTV: The Metric Lenders Now Trust More
entry.summary: Debt yield has quietly displaced LTV as the primary underwriting metric for CRE lenders. How to calculate it, lender minimums by type, and what it means for sponsors sizing their next deal.

entry.title: The Bridge Lending Landscape in 2026: Who's Lending, at What Terms, and Why It Matters
entry.summary: A ground-level look at the 2026 bridge lending landscape: which debt funds, regional banks, and life companies are most active, how terms have changed since 2022, and what sponsors need to know to secure the best execution on transitional assets.

entry.title: Q1 2026 CRE Debt Market Outlook
entry.summary: Analysis of Q1 2026 commercial real estate debt market conditions: rate stabilization, regional bank lending recovery, and what it means for sponsors seeking financing. The commercial real estate debt market is showing clear signs of stabilization as we enter Q1 2026.

entry.title: Bridge vs. Permanent Financing: A Decision Framework
entry.summary: When to use bridge financing versus permanent debt for commercial real estate. A structured decision framework for sponsors evaluating short-term vs long-term capital. Four questions that determine which path is right for your deal.

entry.title: Why Capital Structure Matters More Than Rate
entry.summary: Rate shopping is costing CRE sponsors millions. Why capital structure â€” prepayment flexibility, IO periods, recourse, and certainty of close â€” matter far more than chasing the lowest interest rate.
